clinical_question: Is metformin effective for glycemic control in type 2 diabetes?

summary.top_article: Systematic review: oral hypoglycemics in T2DM — 42 RCTs

assessments.title: Systematic review: oral hypoglycemics in T2DM — 42 RCTs
assessments.main_finding: Metformin showed the best glycemic efficacy with HbA1c reduction of 1.1% (95% CI: 0.9-1.3%)

assessments.title: Metformin vs placebo in T2DM: A randomized controlled trial (n=500)
assessments.main_finding: Metformin reduced HbA1c by 1.2% compared to 0.1% with placebo

pico.population: Patients aged 40-70 with newly diagnosed type 2 diabetes mellitus

assessments.title: Long-term metformin use and cardiovascular outcomes: cohort study (n=12,000)
assessments.main_finding: Metformin users showed 18% lower cardiovascular mortality compared to non-users

assessments.title: Metformin adherence patterns in elderly patients: observational study
assessments.main_finding: Metformin adherence was 62% at 6 months, with GI side effects and polypharmacy being main barriers

pico.population: Elderly patients with type 2 diabetes (age >75) in a single center

assessments.title: Case report: lactic acidosis with metformin in renal impairment
assessments.main_finding: The patient developed metformin-associated lactic acidosis, which was resolved after hemodialysis

pico.population: A 68-year-old patient with type 2 diabetes and chronic kidney disease stage 3
